heading: Considerations
body:
After you enable a Backup Compliance Policy, the following behaviors apply:

- A Backup Compliance Policy limits your ability to reduce backup storage costs. You
  can't adjust the retention or delete a backup to reduce the
  backup storage costs. To learn more,
  see [Billing Cost Explorer](atlas-billing).

- All new and existing clusters have Cloud Backup automatically
  enabled and use the project-level Backup Compliance Policy. Atlas augments any
  preexisting [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy) to meet the minimum requirements of the
  Backup Compliance Policy. All new clusters use the Backup Compliance Policy unless the minimum
  requirements of the [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy) expand beyond the minimum requirements of
  the Backup Compliance Policy.

- You can modify the [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy) at any time. If you reduce the frequency
  of a cluster-level backup policy, the change applies only to
  future backups. Any existing [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) remains for the original
  window. The minimum requirements of the Backup Compliance Policy apply.

- If the Backup Compliance Policy has the
  **Keep all snapshots when removing additional snapshot regions**
  option set to **On** and you enable, modify, or
  delete [snapshot copy distribution](snapshot-distribution), any
  snapshots already taken remain.

- When you [resume](resume-cluster), Atlas
  automatically enables Cloud Backup. If the Backup Compliance Policy has
  the **Require Point in Time Restore to all clusters** option
  set to **On**, Atlas automatically enables
  Continuous Cloud Backup and adjusts the restore window according to the
  Backup Compliance Policy. Atlas automatically modifies the backup to meet the
  minimum requirements of the Backup Compliance Policy.

- If you [Terminate active clusters](terminate-cluster), Atlas
  maintains all existing snapshots after the termination according to
  your backup policy. Atlas retains the [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) for
  [restoring a point in time with continuous cloud backup](recover-pit-continuous-cloud-backup) in a static state until
  Atlas can no longer use them for continuous cloud backup.

- If you [Terminate active clusters](terminate-cluster), you can't create another
  cluster with the same name because Atlas uses the name to
  identify snapshots.

- Whenever a user enables, modifies, or disables a Backup Compliance Policy, Atlas
  reflects the event in the [Project Activity Feed](view-activity-feed).
